name: Rabbit Stew in White Wine Sauce (Pressure Cooker)
recipeIngredient: 1 medium green bell pepper, cut into 1-inch squares
3 medium tomatoes, blanch, peel, and chop
1 package instant chicken broth and seasoning mix
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart pressure cooker, heat 1 tablespoon oil. Add rabbit in batches until evenly browned on all sides. Set aside.
2. Add remaining tablespoon oil and heat. Add onion and garlic. Sauté until onion is soft.
3. Add green pepper and mushrooms; continue sautéing until peppers are tender-crisp.
4. Sprinkle vegetable mixture with flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes longer.
5. Stir in tomatoes and wine; bring to a boil. Add water, broth mix, thyme, salt, pepper, and browned rabbit. Stir to combine.
6. Securely close the cover. Place the pressure regulator on the vent pipe and heat until it begins to rock gently.
7. Cook at 15 pounds pressure for 15 minutes.
8. Remove the cooker from heat and let the pressure release naturally.
9. Transfer rabbit to a serving dish. Cook the sauce over high heat until slightly thickened, about 2 minutes. Pour sauce over rabbit and sprinkle with parsley.
